vintageimprovements.item_description.add: Another part of this machine has a different minimum RPM requirement:
vintageimprovements.item_description.mac: This machine has a minimum RPM requirement:
vintageimprovements.ponder.belt_grinder_: Processing Items on the Belt Grinder
Belt Grinders can process a variety of items
The processed item always moves against the rotational input to the grinder
Some recipes requires a specific RPM
There are three speeds: Low (16 RPM or less), Medium (between 16 and 64 RPM) and High (over 64 RPM)
vintageimprovements.ponder.centrifuge.he: Processing Items & Fluids in the Centrifuge
vintageimprovements.ponder.centrifuge.te: The Centrifuge can process a variety of items and fluids
Before use you must install 4 Basins on the Centrifuge
It can be powered from the top or bottom using shafts
Items and Fluids can only be inserted when the Centrifuge is stopped
The result can be extracted via right-click or automatic extraction...
...but only when the Centrifuge is stopped
vintageimprovements.ponder.centrifuge_re: The Centrifuge can produce Comparator signal
You must install a Redstone Module into the Centrifuge
The Centrifuge will produce a signal strength of 15 as long as it has a recipe
vintageimprovements.ponder.curving_press: Processing Items with the Curving Press
Before work you must install any Curving Head to Curving Press
To remove head, you must right-click with a Wrench
The Curving Press can process items provided beneath it
The Input items can be dropped or placed on a Depot under the Press
When items are provided by a belt...
The Press will hold and process them automatically
vintageimprovements.ponder.curving_redst: Curving Press can produce Comparator signal
You must install Redstone Module into the Curving Press
Curving Press will produce a level 15 redstone signal as long as it has an installed head
Processing Items with the Helve Hammer
The Helve Hammer has two operating modes
The mode depends on block under Hammer
The two blocks are: Anvil and Smithing Table
With a Smithing Table, the Helve Hammer will process any Smithing recipes
With an Anvil, the Helve Hammer will process special Hammering recipes
It can be powered from the side using shafts
Throw or Insert items on top of the Anvil or Smithing Table
After a set amount of hammer blows, the result can be obtained by right-clicking
Items can also be extracted/inserted by automation
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_1: The Laser consumes energy during works (only if another mod that provides energy is installed)
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_2: Any sources of Forge Energy are supported
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_3: The Laser can process items provided beneath it
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_4: The Input items can be dropped or placed on a Depot under the Laser
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_5: When items are provided by a belt...
vintageimprovements.ponder.laser.text_6: The Laser will hold and process them automatically
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_1: Lathe can process a variety of items
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_2: They must be powered from the side...
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_4: Ingredients can be inserted to front block via Right-click
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_5: To select a recipe, click on the back block
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_6: You need to do this every time
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_7: The result can be obtained via Right-click
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe.text_8: The items can also be extracted/inserted by automation
vintageimprovements.ponder.lathe_automat: Lathe can be automated with the Recipe Card
Use a Recipe Card via Right-click to define a recipe
Then you must put it inside a back Lathe block
Lathe will automatically apply chosen recipe
vintageimprovements.ponder.slots_blockin: Some recipes may require less then 3 ingredients
You can block redundant slots with Helve Hammer Slot Cover
You can remove Slot Covers via right-click with a Wrench
vintageimprovements.ponder.spring_coilin: Processing Items on the Spring Coiling Machine
Spring Coiling Machines can process a variety of items
The processed item is always ejected out the front of the machine
vintageimprovements.ponder.vacuum_chambe: The Compressor has two operating modes that can be changed by right clicking it with a Wrench
Some recipes may require the heat of a Blaze Burner
Some recipes output fluids that appear inside Compressor block
To drain fluids you must use a Mechanical Pump
Some recipes require fluids inside the Compressor
You can fill the Compressor using a Mechanical Pump
vintageimprovements.ponder.vibrating_tab: Processing Items on the Vibrating Table
The Vibrating Table can process a variety of items
It can also unpack storage blocks
Or vibrate leaves to get matched drops, drop rates being the same as using Hoe with Fortune III
It can be powered from the side using shafts
Throw or Insert items on the top
After some time, the result can be obtained by right-clicking
The outputs can also be extracted via automation
